title: Updated consensus terminology and definitions in chest electrical impedance tomography
one_line_summary: This TREND group consensus updates and extends the 2017 chest EIT terminology, systematically categorizing measures based on ventilation-related, heartbeat-related, and contrast-enhanced signals to unify reporting and analysis.
key_pearls: Electrical impedance tomography (EIT) is used primarily for assessing regional lung ventilation, aeration changes, and respiratory mechanics, with growing application in imaging regional lung perfusion and cardiac function.
A decremental PEEP trial with EIT analysis of the crossing point between overdistension and collapse curves (OD-CL) can individualize PEEP selection to minimize both overdistension and collapse.
Ventilation-related EIT measures include tidal impedance variation, centre of ventilation, global inhomogeneity index, regional ventilation delay, and expiratory time constants.
Heartbeat-related and contrast-enhanced EIT methods allow quantification of regional lung perfusion and ventilation-perfusion matching, especially when combined with ventilation images.
Standardized EIT terminology, image types, and measurement definitions are **essential** for consistent study design, data analysis, reporting, and cross-device comparability.
EIT provides continuous bedside, radiation-free functional imaging with high temporal resolution but lower spatial resolution (~10% of chest diameter) compared with CT or MRI.
A comprehensive set of equations for calculating EIT-derived parameters is provided to enhance uniformity across research and clinical centers.
consensus_method: Expert consensus by the TRanslational EIT development stuDy (TREND) group members, in cooperation with EIT technology producers.

recommendation_blocks.topic: General EIT terminology and ventilation maneuvers (Tables 1 & 2)
recommendation_blocks.narrative:
- **Absolute EIT (aEIT)**: EIT mode calculating each image from a single data frame; not yet clinical.
- **Aeration change**: Change in regional lung aeration due to varying air content, e.g., from **PEEP** changes.
- **Baseline (BL)**: Reference measurement for time-difference EIT; choice impacts all derived parameters.
- **Chest EIT**: Electrode placement on the chest to assess regional lung ventilation, aeration, heart action, or perfusion.
- **Current stimulation frequency**: Alternating drive current frequency, typically **10 kHz – 250 kHz**.
- **Adjacent stimulation pattern**: Sequential current through adjacent electrode pairs; focuses measurement to boundary.
- **Opposite stimulation pattern**: Sequential current through opposite pairs; more homogeneous sensitivity.
- **EIT data frame**: Complete voltage measurements from one cycle of current stimulations; used to reconstruct one primary image.
- **Electrode-skin contact impedance**: Should be low to ensure high-quality recordings; continuously monitored.
- **Frequency-difference EIT (fdEIT)**: Images show difference in tissue properties between two or more stimulation frequencies.
- **Impedance (Z)**: Opposition to alternating current; air-filled lungs have high impedance, fluids low.
- **Scan rate (frame rate)**: Frequency of scans; typically **20-50 Hz** for ventilation, higher for cardiac events.
- **Signal quality**: Indicator of EIT data accuracy; impaired by poor contact, movement, interference.
- **Ventilation maneuvers**:
   - *Incremental/decremental airway pressure step*: Step change to assess regional filling/emptying dynamics and time constants.
   - *Breath holding (apnoea)*: Used to isolate heartbeat-related signal or for contrast bolus injection.
   - *Forced or slow full lung deflation/inflation*: Regional analogs of **FVC**, **FEV1**, flow, and timing from spirometry.
   - *Decremental PEEP trial*: Stepwise PEEP reduction; EIT identifies overdistension (compliance gain) and collapse (compliance loss) to determine individualized **'optimal PEEP'**.
   - *Incremental PEEP trial*: Stepwise PEEP increase; used less commonly but shows aeration change distribution.

recommendations.statement: Users should adopt the standardized general EIT terminology and execute ventilation maneuvers as defined in this consensus to improve uniformity of data acquisition and reporting.

recommendation_blocks.topic: Types of EIT images and image-related terminology (Table 3)
recommendation_blocks.narrative:
- **Aeration change image (ΔEELZ image)**: Shows regional change in lung impedance (end-expiratory lung volume change) between two time points.
- **Absolute EIT image**: Impedance distribution from a single frame; requires accurate geometry models; not in current clinical devices.
- **Combined / Superimposed EIT image**: EIT functional image overlaid on CT or another EIT image; provides anatomical context or multi-parameter fusion.
- **Curvilinearity image**: Displays 2nd-degree polynomial coefficient from pixel vs. global EIT signal; identifies regions of decelerating or accelerating air filling.
- **Dynamic image (EIT movie)**: Real-time sequence of relative impedance changes, updated at the scan rate.
- **Global EIT waveform**: Sum or average of pixel values across the entire plane; used for reference timing.
- **Heartbeat-related impedance variation image (Pulsatility image)**: Distribution of impedance changes synchronous with heart rate; reflects blood volume changes in heart and large vessels.
- **Images of regional compliance change (OD-CL images)**: From decremental PEEP trial; show compliance loss towards higher PEEP (overdistension) and towards lower PEEP (collapse).
- **Linear regression image (Slope image)**: Shows slope of linear fit between regional and global EIT signal; identifies ventilated regions.
- **Normalized tidal impedance variation image**: Pixel tidal values normalized to maximum; pixels with <10% of max ("**silent spaces**") may indicate overdistension or atelectasis.
- **Pendelluft image**: Highlights regions with reverse air flow during inspiration, indicative of intrapulmonary gas redistribution.
- **Perfusion image**: Derived from contrast-enhanced EIT; shows distribution of pulmonary blood flow; not to be confused with pulsatility image.
- **Phase image**: Displays phase shift between regional and global EIT signals; identifies temporal heterogeneity of air filling.
- **Regional ventilation delay (RVD) image**: Based on time to reach a preset percentage of maximum pixel impedance during inflation; used during low-flow maneuvers.
- **Tidal impedance variation image (TIV)**: Most common ventilation image; pixel values reflect regional tidal volume distribution.
- **Ventilation-perfusion (V̇/Q) image**: Combines ventilation and perfusion images; can categorize regions of shunt, dead space, and matched V̇/Q.
- **Region-of-interest (ROI) conventions**: Ventral (ROI 1), mid-ventral (ROI 2), mid-dorsal (ROI 3), dorsal (ROI 4) for horizontal layers; quadrants numbered clockwise from upper left.
- **Image orientation**: Conventional radiological view (viewing from feet, ventral at top, right side on left of image).

recommendations.statement: The **recommended** image types and ROI definitions should be used to standardize visualization and quantification of EIT data.

recommendation_blocks.narrative:
- **Tidal impedance variation (TIV)**: **Absolute** difference between end-expiratory and end-inspiratory impedance; calculated globally, regionally, or per pixel.
- **Global inhomogeneity (GI) index**: Sum of **absolute** differences between median and individual pixel TIV within lung region, normalized to total; higher = more spatial heterogeneity.
- **Centre of ventilation (CoV)**: Geometric centre of TIV distribution; ventrodorsal (CoVvd) reflects ventral-to-dorsal shift ( >50% = dorsal); right-to-left (CoVrl) detects unilateral changes.
- **Ventilation ratio, ventrodorsal (V/D)**: Ratio of summed TIV in ventral half to dorsal half; posture-dependent.
- **Ventilation ratio, right-to-left (R/L)**: Ratio of right lung TIV to left lung TIV.
- **Fraction of ventilation**: Regional TIV divided by global TIV; expressed as percentage.
- **Ventilation defect score**: Categorical score (0–8) based on TIV fractions in four quadrants; higher = more asymmetry.
- **Ventilated lung area**: Number of pixels with TIV above a threshold (e.g., 20% of max).
- **Change in end-expiratory lung impedance (ΔEELZ)**: Global or regional difference in EELI between time points; positive = increased aeration.
- **Coefficient of variation (CV)**: Ratio of standard deviation to mean of pixel TIV; measure of spatial heterogeneity.
- **Phase angle**: From cross-spectra; positive = earlier filling; negative = delayed filling relative to global.
- **Intratidal volume distribution**: Relative contribution of image regions to impedance change during 8 equal volume fractions of inspiration.
- **Linearity/Curvilinearity**: Linear regression coefficient (slope) and 2nd-order polynomial coefficient from pixel vs. global EIT curves; characterizes filling pattern.
- **Filling index (FI)**: Exponent in power-law fit Zpixel = a*Zglobal^FI; FI >1 = slow initial filling (tidal recruitment); <1 = fast initial filling.
- **Regional ventilation delay (RVD)**: Time to reach **40%** of maximum pixel impedance, normalized to inspiratory time; RVDI is standard deviation of pixel RVD.
- **Pendelluft**: Visualized by reverse flow; quantified by time differences between pixel and global inspiratory/expiratory peaks or by **fraction of reverse impedance change (FRIC)**.
- **Expiration time (tE)**: Time to exhale 25%, 50%, 75%, 90% of pixel forced vital capacity; derived from forced expiration maneuvers.
- **Regional time constant (τ)**: Obtained by exponential fitting of passive deflation after pressure step; single- or multi-breath.
- **Regional respiratory system compliance (Crs)**: Regional TIV divided by driving pressure; used during PEEP trial to identify overdistension and collapse.
- **Overdistension (OD) and collapse (CL) curves**: Plots of fraction of pixels with compliance increase (OD) and decrease (CL) vs PEEP; their **crossing point** guides PEEP selection.
- **Regional opening pressure (ROP) / closing pressure (RCP)**: Airway pressure at which pixel impedance reaches a threshold (e.g., 10%) during low-flow inflation/deflation.
- **Recruitment-to-inflation ratio (R/I)**: EIT-based index comparing measured and predicted end-expiratory lung volume change after PEEP drop to estimate recruitability.
- **Atelectrauma index (AtI)**: Fraction of ventilated pixels with identifiable lower inflection points on both inflation and deflation limbs during quasi-static P-V maneuver.

recommendations.statement: The primary **recommended** terms and calculation methods for ventilation-related EIT measures should be used to ensure comparability of study results.

recommendation_blocks.narrative:
- **Heartbeat-related impedance variation**: Global or regional signal synchronous with heart rate; reflects blood volume changes in heart and large vessel pulsatility.
- **Cardiac filtering**: Band-pass filtering or ECG-gated averaging to separate heartbeat-related from ventilation-related signal.
- **Contrast-enhanced EIT**: Bolus injection of **hypertonic saline (≤10%)** via central venous line to track indicator passage through right heart, pulmonary circulation, left heart, and aorta; allows perfusion imaging.
- **Lung perfusion (Q)**: Regional blood flow derived from first-pass kinetics of contrast agent; quantified by **gamma-variate area** or **maximum slope** methods.
- **Perfusion image**: Pixel distribution of Q; combined with ventilation image yields **V̇/Q image**.
- **Fraction of perfusion**: Regional perfusion divided by total perfusion.
- **Centre of perfusion (CoP)**: Geometric centre of perfusion distribution; analogous to CoV.
- **Centre of heart (CoH)**: Centre of saline distribution in right heart during contrast passage; right-to-left shift may indicate intracardiac shunt.
- **Stroke volume (SV)**: Volume pumped per heartbeat; estimated from heartbeat-related impedance or contrast dilution.
- **Cardiac output (CO)**: SV × heart rate; experimental estimation by EIT.
- **Pulse transit time (PTT) / Pulse arrival time (PAT)**: Time for pressure wave to travel between two arterial sites; EIT-derived PTT as surrogate for **pulmonary artery pressure (PAP)**.
- **Stroke volume variation (SSVEIT)**: Ventilation-induced modulation of aortic EIT signal; investigated for fluid responsiveness.

recommendations.statement: The **recommended** terminology for heartbeat-related and contrast-enhanced EIT measures should be applied, with preference for contrast-based methods when assessing regional lung perfusion.

strengths_limitations:
- Strengths: Comprehensive update covering all current EIT applications; systematic taxonomy of measures; extensive reference to founding and clinical studies; inclusion of calculation equations; multinational expert consensus.
- Limitations: EIT spatial resolution (~10% chest diameter) is lower than CT/MRI; measures remain in arbitrary units unless calibrated; signal quality dependent on electrode contact and patient movement; some cardiovascular measures lack robust clinical validation; no formal evidence grading.
